title: The Bridge Builder
description: Choose the correct connector to join the two sentences.

questions.question: I wanted to go out, ___ it was raining.
questions.explanation: "But" expresses contrast between wanting to go out and the rain.

questions.question: She studied hard. ___, she passed the exam.
I stayed home ___ I was feeling sick.
questions.explanation: "Because" indicates the reason for staying home.

questions.question: ___ it was expensive, she bought the dress.
questions.explanation: "Although" expresses contrast: despite it being expensive, he bought it.

questions.question: ___, preheat the oven to 180°C.
questions.explanation: "First" indicates the first step in a sequence.

questions.question: The movie was long. ___, it was very entertaining.
questions.explanation: "However" expresa contraste formal entre largo y entretenido.

questions.question: I love chocolate, ___ I try not to eat it every day.
questions.explanation: "But" contrasts the love of chocolate with restriction.

questions.question: It was raining, ___ I took an umbrella.
questions.explanation: "So" indicates result: it was raining → I took an umbrella.

questions.question: She speaks English. She ___ speaks French and German.
___ mixing all the ingredients, bake for 30 minutes.

sentences.prompt: Connect: 'I like coffee' + 'I don't drink it often'
sentences.correct: I like coffee, however, I don't drink it often

sentences.prompt: Express cause: why you stayed home
sentences.correct: I stayed home because I was sick

sentences.prompt: Give a sequence: first step of a recipe
Express result: it was cold → wore a jacket
sentences.correct: It was cold, so I wore a jacket
Although she was tired, she kept working

title: Connector → Function
description: Match each connector with its communicative function.

title: Connectors Flashcards
description: Learn each connector with its category, use and example.

cards.back: However / However — Connect contrasting ideas. More formal than "but."
cards.example: The movie was long. However, it was very entertaining.

cards.back: Therefore — Indicates a logical conclusion or result.
cards.example: She studied hard. Therefore, she passed the exam.

cards.back: Additionally — Add information with emphasis. Formal.
cards.example: The hotel is beautiful. Moreover, it is very affordable.

cards.back: Although — Introduces an idea contrary to what was expected.
cards.example: Although it was raining, we had a great time.

cards.back: Because — Explains the reason for something.
cards.example: I couldn't sleep because it was too noisy.

cards.back: Despite — Similar to although but followed by noun/-ing.
cards.example: Despite the rain, we went to the park.
In addition, we offer free delivery.

cards.back: As a result — Indicates consequence. Formal.
cards.example: The company grew. As a result, they hired more people.

cards.back: Since / Since — Known cause. More formal than because.
cards.example: Since you're already here, let's start.

cards.back: For example — Enter a concrete example.
cards.example: Many fruits are healthy. For instance, oranges have vitamin C.

title: Connectors in English
description: Decipher connectors that organize ideas into deep conversations.

title: The Bridge Builder Listening
description: Listen and choose the correct answer.

items.audioText: I like coffee, however, I don't drink it often
items.question: What did you hear?
options: I like coffee, however, I don't drink it often
I stayed home because I was sick
It was cold, so I wore a jacket
items.explanation: You heard: "I like coffee, however, I don't drink it often".
